seoTitle: Short vs Skye — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Short and Skye. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Short and Skye.
heading: Short vs Skye — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Short, South Australia and Skye, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Skye has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Short (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Short or Skye safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Short has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Skye scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Short scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Skye scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Short or Skye?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Skye has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
